summary: Stage decomp PR for Ice Mountain and Kongo Jungle. It renamed/exposed the Ice Mountain helper as `grIceMt_801F91EC`, fixed its header prototype, used zero animation offsets in its `grAnime_801C7A04` calls, and implemented `grKongo_801D8078` as an item proximity scan over `HSD_GObj_Entities->items`. The automated match report recorded `grIceMt_801F91EC` as a new 100% match and `grKongo_801D8078` as improved to 98.43%, with `gricemt` `.sdata2` also improved.

key_files.role: Renamed `FUN_801f91ec` to `grIceMt_801F91EC`, removed the nonmatching marker, and changed the `grAnime_801C7A04` fourth argument from `-20` to `0`.
Replaced the old `UNK_RET grIceMt_801F91EC(HSD_GObj*)` declaration with the full `void` signature using an `s16*` state block, integer parameters, and `mpLib_Callback`.
Added `it/it_26B1.h` and implemented `grKongo_801D8078` to find a nearby item of kind `0xDA` using item positions and a squared-radius check.

what_changed: `grIceMt_801F91EC` became the actual named function instead of the static placeholder `FUN_801f91ec`.

The Ice Mountain helper writes a seven-slot `s16` state/parameter block, starts animation `param_3`, optionally starts animation `param_4` when it is not `-1`, and installs a joint callback via `mpJointSetCb1(param_5, gp, param_8)`.

The two Ice Mountain animation calls now pass `0` as the fourth argument to `grAnime_801C7A04` instead of `-20`, which the match report correlated with improved `gricemt` matching and `.sdata2` progress.
`gricemt.h` now has the full prototype for `grIceMt_801F91EC`: `void grIceMt_801F91EC(HSD_GObj*, s16*, int, int, int, int, int, mpLib_Callback)`.

`grKongo_801D8078` now calls `Ground_801C4DA0(&pos, &unk)`, iterates `HSD_GObj_Entities->items`, filters by `itGetKind(cur) == 0xDA`, reads item position with `it_8026B294`, and returns the first item whose squared distance from `pos` is less than `grKg_804D6980->unk28 * grKg_804D6980->unk28`.
The Kongo implementation returns `NULL` if no qualifying nearby item is found.
The PR touched 3 files with 43 additions and 12 deletions.
smart_moves: Promoted the known address-named Ice Mountain symbol into the C definition and header instead of leaving a lowercase `FUN_` placeholder and an `UNK_RET` prototype.
Kept the Ice Mountain helper signature typed enough to document cross-system behavior: `HSD_GObj*`, `s16*`, integer animation/joint/timing values, and `mpLib_Callback`.
Used the existing item helpers `itGetKind` and `it_8026B294` rather than reaching into item internals to identify and locate Kongo items.
Used a squared-distance comparison against `r * r`, avoiding a square root and matching the likely original float operation pattern.
Preserved the optional-animation sentinel `param_4 != -1`, which is a reusable pattern for helper functions that may configure one or two stage animation tracks.
decomp_lessons: When the PR body conflicts with the title and diff, prefer the title, changed files, and match report; this PR body appears copied from unrelated `mnname.c`/`toy.c` work.
Fixing a single literal in animation setup can affect both function matching and `.sdata2` matching; here `-20` to `0` was part of the Ice Mountain match improvement.
Stage headers should be updated as soon as a real signature is known; replacing `UNK_RET` and one-argument placeholders makes later call-site and callback decomp cleaner.
Item-search stage helpers often decompile as: get a stage/world position, scan `HSD_GObj_Entities->items`, filter by item kind, fetch item position, then compare squared distance.
Do not mark a function as fully solved just because it has a plausible C body; the automated report still listed `grKongo_801D8078` at 98.43%, so later work should look for the remaining mismatch.
naming_conventions: Ground/stage functions use the stage prefix plus address form, e.g. `grIceMt_801F91EC` and `grKongo_801D8078`.
Use uppercase hex in canonical symbol names and comments, not lowercase placeholder names such as `FUN_801f91ec`.
Keep subsystem includes explicit: this PR added `it/it_26B1.h` for item helper declarations while retaining stage includes such as `gr/stage.h`.
The PR did not provide semantic names for the Ice Mountain parameters; generic `param_1` through `param_8` remained, which is appropriate when evidence for better names is weak.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For `grIceMt_801F91EC`, call `grAnime_801C7A04(param_1, anim_id, 7, 0)`; using `-20` produced the wrong match behavior in this slice.
Write the `s16` state block slots explicitly before starting animations and installing the mpLib callback.
Use the `param_4 != -1` branch shape for optional second animation setup.
For `grKongo_801D8078`, scan the item linked list with `for (cur = HSD_GObj_Entities->items; cur != NULL; cur = cur->next)`.
Keep distance math scalarized as `dx`, `dy`, `dz`, squared temporaries, and `dx2 + dy2 + dz2 < r * r`; this is a useful float-ordering tactic for proximity checks.
The PR's Kongo function was only reported at 98.43%, so later matching may need stack/temporary/order refinements beyond the high-level logic shown here.
review_feedback: No human review comments were captured for this PR.
Automated report by `encounter`: `main/melee/gr/gricemt` item `grIceMt_801F91EC` became a new 100.00% match for +176 bytes.
Automated report by `encounter`: `main/melee/gr/grkongo` item `grKongo_801D8078` improved from 0.00% to 98.43% for +185 bytes.
Automated report by `encounter`: `main/melee/gr/gricemt` `.sdata2` improved from 21.05% to 62.12% for +33 bytes.
The submitted PR body mentions `IsNameUnique` in `mnname.c` and `un_80305D00` in `toy.c`, which is inconsistent with the title, diff, and match report; treat that body text as stale or unrelated.
